title: roldana hederoides
intro: Roldana hederoides, also known as Mexican false ageratum, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. It is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin irritations. The plant's leaves and stems can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it a valuable natural remedy for wounds and infections. Regular consumption of Roldana hederoides herbal preparations may also provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting the immune system.
